Append sheets to an existing Excel file

append_excel.Rd

Adds one or more sheets to an existing .xlsx workbook without touching its other sheets. New sheets get the same formatting as write_excel(): bold frozen header, blank NA cells, and #,##0 number format for large numeric columns (with automatic exclusion of year-like columns).

Usage

append_excel(
  data,
  path,
  overwrite = FALSE,
  as_table = FALSE,
  freeze_header = TRUE,
  skip_fmt = NULL,
  detect_years = TRUE
)

Arguments

data

A data frame or named list of data frames to add as new sheets. Same naming defaults as in write_excel().

path

Path to an existing .xlsx file.

overwrite

If FALSE (default), aborts when a sheet of the same name already exists in the workbook. Set to TRUE to replace it.

as_table

If TRUE, data is inserted as an Excel ListObject (table with filter arrows and banded rows). Default FALSE.

freeze_header

If TRUE (default), freezes the first row.

skip_fmt

Character vector of column names to exclude from the #,##0 number format.

detect_years

If TRUE (default), numeric columns where every non-NA value is a whole number between 1800 and 2200 are excluded from the #,##0 number format.

Value

path, invisibly.

See also

write_excel()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
write_excel(list(Hoved = mtcars), "out.xlsx")
append_excel(list(Bilag = iris), "out.xlsx")
} # }
